source_pr.title: ddl: add states to remove old objects during modifying column
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: close #60264, close #62531 Problem Summary: ```sql -- origin schema create table t (a int primary key, b varchar(255), key k_b (b)) -- ddl alter table t modify column b int unsigned not null; -- write-reorg -> public -- non-owner schema (_Col$ and _Idx$ are not visible to users) create table t (a int primary key, b int, _Col$_b varchar(255), key k_b (b), key _Idx$_k_b (_Col$_b)); -- owner schema create table t (a int primary key, b int, key k_b (b)); -- owner insert (OLD INDEX IS NOT MAINTAINED!) insert into t values (10, " 09.60 "); -- non-owner select select * from t where a = 10; 10 -- non-owner admin check admin check table t; [admin:8223]data inconsistency in table: t, index: k_b, handle: 10, index-values:"" != record-values:"handle: 10, values: [KindNull ]" ``` The root cause is that the assumption of "two adjacent schema versions are compatible" is broken for modifying columns covered with indexes: - Non-owner TiDB has schema version `n-1`. Only old indexes and old columns are visible. - Owner TiDB has schema version `n`. Only new indexes are visible. When owner TiDB inserts or deletes a record, only new indexes will be maintained. If you run `SELECT` or `ADMIN CHECK` on non-owner TiDB with schema version `n-1`, either the data has one more record than the index, or the index has one more record than the data, depending on whether the non-owner node executes an insert or delete statement. This issue doesn't happen on `ADD INDEX` because non-owner TiDBs don't have an "old" index. Any `SELECT` statements will use `TableScan` instead of `IndexLookup`. There is no such chance to see inconsistent data on non-owner TiDBs. ### What changed and how does it work? Before removing public columns/indexes, we should follow the path `public` -> `write-only` -> `delete-only` -> `none`. In the above case, **owner TiDB is still aware of the old index** (which is in `write-only` state). As long as it maintains the old index mutations, there is no data inconsistent issue. - Break changing column step `write-reorg` -> `public` into multiple steps: - changing column `write-reorg`, old column & indexes `public` - changing column `public`, old column & indexes `write-only` - changing column `public`, old column & indexes `delete-only` - changing column `public`, old column & indexes `none`(removed) - Don't use column name to locate "old column" anymore. This PR changes to use column ID, and it is persisted to job arguments after the first execution. - Add an internal column / index name prefix `_Tomestone$_` to represent the objects that need to be removed eventually. Example 1 ```sql create table t (a int not null default 1, b int default 2, c int not null default 0, primary key(c), index idx(b), index idx1(a), index idx2(b, c)); alter table t change column b bb mediumint first; ``` Example 2 ```sql create table t (a int, b int, c int, index i1(a), index i2(b), index i3(c), index i4(a, b), index i5(a, b, c)); alter table t modify column a tinyint, modify column b tinyint, modify column c tinyint; ``` ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) Run the [script](https://github.com/pingyu/ddl-and-delete/blob/main/main.go) mentioned in #62531 for 20 min, no data inconsistent issue found. - [ ] No need to test > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed. > Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility Documentation - [ ] Affects user behaviors - [ ] Contains syntax changes - [ ] Contains variable changes - [ ] Contains experimental features - [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: Before removing public columns/indexes, we should follow the path `public` -> `write-only` -> `delete-only` -> `none`.
fix_pattern: Before removing public columns/indexes, we should follow the path `public` -> `write-only` -> `delete-only` -> `none`. In the above case, **owner TiDB is still aware of the old index** (which is in `write-only` state). As long as it maintains the old index mutations, there is no data inconsistent issue.
